{"step_by_step_thinking": "Tocilizumab is not an anti-TNF antibody. It is a monoclonal antibody that targets the interleukin-6 (IL-6) receptor, not tumor necrosis factor (TNF). The documents confirm that tocilizumab is used in conditions involving IL-6-mediated inflammation, such as rheumatoid arthritis and COVID-19, and it is specifically described as an IL-6 inhibitor. In contrast, anti-TNF antibodies (like infliximab, adalimumab, etanercept) target TNF-alpha. The UMLS documents do not classify tocilizumab as an anti-TNF agent, and the MEDCORP documents do not list it as such. Therefore, the statement that tocilizumab is an anti-TNF antibody is false.", "answer_choice": "B"}
